reforms.description: The government has introduced a system of innovation vouchers that can be used by small and medium-sized enterprises to buy knowledge from public knowledge institutes, alongside extending WBSO tax breaks for SMEs to stimulate private sector R&D and innovation.
reforms.package_name: Innovation Voucher and Tax Breaks Initiative
reforms.component_name: Innovation Vouchers and WBSO Tax Breaks
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The initiative enhances demand-driven research and stimulates private sector innovation, which is expected to raise productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been put into practice and are actively supporting SMEs.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of innovation vouchers and tax breaks represents a significant but narrower reform aimed at enhancing innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The government aims to strengthen linkages between public research organisations (PRO) and firms in order to increase private R&D.

reforms.description: The government has rationalised its financial support for R&D activity to improve coordination among various agencies and is reallocating part of the university research budget to incentivize universities to cooperate more with firms in innovation activities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improved coordination and targeted funding are expected to enhance the effectiveness of public funding for innovation and foster collaboration between universities and firms.
reforms.status_evidence: The rationalisation and reallocation of funds have been executed and are currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant adjustment in funding strategy aimed at enhancing innovation collaboration.
reforms.source_quote: A small step towards making university budgets more dependent on co-operation with firms in innovation activities is being taken by reallocating part of the university research budget (€100 million out of a total budget of €2 billion).

reforms.description: The Dutch government initiated the 'Andere Overheid' programme aimed at simplifying administrative procedures and established a Taskforce to simplify licence and permit procedures, particularly focusing on reducing the complexity and costs associated with starting a business.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Simplifying administrative procedures is expected to lower barriers for startups, thereby enhancing entrepreneurship and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme and Taskforce have been established and are actively working to simplify procedures.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant reform aimed at improving the business environment for startups.
reforms.source_quote: The Andere Overheid programme focuses on less regulation, better public services, greater efficiency and better cooperation with private organisations.

reforms.description: The Dutch government established the TechnoPartner Action Programme to streamline existing instruments supporting technology-based start-ups and plans to reform bankruptcy law to reduce personal costs for bankrupt entrepreneurs, facilitating a quicker restart for non-fraudulent bankrupts, while also enhancing public support for innovative firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The initiative aims to enhance the growth potential of the startup ecosystem and facilitate entrepreneurship by reducing barriers and risks associated with failure.
reforms.status_evidence: The TechnoPartner Action Programme has been established and bankruptcy law reforms are in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of the TechnoPartner Action Programme and significant bankruptcy law reforms represent a major overhaul of support for startups.
reforms.source_quote: A new law on bankruptcy will be available at the end of 2005. As is the government’s intention, this law should reduce the personal costs of bankruptcy and the options for a swift re-start of non-fraudulent bankrupts.

reforms.description: The Dutch government introduced the smart mix policy instrument to enhance focus on excellent basic research and its social and economic valorisation through collaborative programmes.
reforms.package_name: Smart Mix Policy for Basic Research
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The focus on excellent basic research and its valorisation is expected to contribute to long-term innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The policy instrument has been introduced but not yet fully implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of the smart mix policy represents a significant adjustment in funding strategy for basic research.
reforms.source_quote: A new policy instrument (the smart mix) will be launched in 2006 that provides for programmes to enhance focus and mass in excellent basic research and social and economic valorisation of this research.

reforms.description: The Dutch government is shifting its innovation support towards specific instruments to enhance the external benefits of its R&D budget, focusing on key fields such as genomics, ICT, and nanotechnology.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Focusing on key fields is expected to maximize the impact of R&D investments and drive innovation in critical sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The shift in support instruments has been enacted and is currently operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The shift in innovation support represents a major reform in how R&D budgets are allocated and utilized.
reforms.source_quote: The Dutch government is moving more of its support for innovation towards specific instruments.

reforms.description: The Ministry of Economic Affairs plans to rationalise the list of support instruments for entrepreneurship, which includes various innovation instruments such as Knowledge Transfer Subsidy Schemes and Innovation Vouchers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The impact of the rationalisation is not yet clear as it is still in the planning phase.
reforms.status_evidence: The rationalisation has been announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The planned rationalisation represents a significant overhaul of the support system for entrepreneurship.
reforms.source_quote: The MEA plans to rationalise the list of support instruments.

reforms.description: The Netherlands aims to enhance innovation activity by removing barriers to competition and disincentives to entrepreneurship, which will contribute to productivity growth, particularly in ICT-using service sectors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Removing barriers is expected to foster a more competitive environment that encourages innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative has been proposed but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant effort to improve the innovation landscape.
reforms.source_quote: Removing barriers to competition and enhancing innovation activity to raise productivity growth.

reforms.description: The introduction of TechnoPartner, a generic tax facility for innovative startups collaborating with universities, aims to enhance cooperation and support for innovation in the startup ecosystem.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax facility is designed to encourage collaboration between startups and universities, fostering innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The TechnoPartner tax facility has been introduced and is currently operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of the TechnoPartner tax facility represents a significant but narrower reform aimed at supporting startups.
reforms.source_quote: Introduce TechnoPartner, the generic tax facility for innovative start-ups co-operating with universities.

reforms.description: The government established a separate Fund for Improvement of the Structure of the Economy (FES) to ensure that revenues from natural gas resources are used for investments that strengthen the economy's structure, targeting innovation and infrastructure.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in innovation and infrastructure is expected to strengthen the economy's structure and promote long-term growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The fund has been announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of the FES represents a major reform in how economic resources are allocated towards innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Over 40% of the gas resources flow into the FES, and should subsequently only be used for investments that strengthen the structure of the economy.
